title: Pattern Spec (backend-neutral IR)
description: Authoritative intermediate representation that backends compile into a concrete harness. Separates the invariant pattern from instance-specific values.

spec_version.description: Version of this spec schema the document targets.

intent.description: One or two sentences: the invariant pattern, stated without any instance values.

domain.description: Optional domain label, e.g. 'scheduling'. Used for harness output path.

task.description: Optional task slug. Used for harness output path.

input_contract.description: The shape of inputs, with each part explicitly marked variable or fixed.

type.description: Logical type, e.g. string, number, date, array, object, file.

variability.description: variable = changes per run (a harness input dimension); fixed = constant across runs.

example.description: Illustrative value. For 'fixed' fields this is the constant; for 'variable' fields this is one sample only and MUST NOT be hardcoded in the harness.

steps.description: Ordered processing steps, each tagged deterministic or semantic.

kind.description: deterministic = reproducible by code; semantic = needs a constrained model call or agent step.

rules.description: Constraints the output must satisfy (e.g. resource-availability, ordering).

output_contract.description: Pointer to the concrete per-task output schema and a human summary.

schema_ref.description: Relative path to this task's output.schema.json (authoritative for harness output validation).

acceptance_criteria.description: What made the output good — the user's answer to the one focused question. Each criterion is tagged structural (deterministic assert) or qualitative (judge subagent).

check_type.description: structural = field equality/ordering/length/schema conformance (must hold on perturbed inputs); qualitative = LLM-as-judge.

semantic_steps.description: Ids of steps that are irreducibly semantic. Must be a subset of steps[].id with kind=semantic. Empty array is allowed (pure-deterministic pattern).

harness_type.description: deterministic = no real semantic step; hybrid = scaffold + 1-2 schema-validated model calls; agentic = open-ended agent loop reproduced as a recipe.

target_runtime.description: Chosen from harness_type plus the user's ecosystem.
